summary: Frontend re-test on Expo Web (mobile viewport 390x844) of the 2 HIGH bugs reported in iteration 1. Both fixes verified end-to-end with a full client→driver E2E run: cliente@chekou.com created an order in Supermercado Tosta 2 (R$ 30 estimated), paid via mock payment, captured the 4-digit confirmation code from /client/tracking; then entregador@chekou.com logged in, the level strip rendered correctly, accepted the order, advanced status through 'Indo ao estabelecimento' → 'Comprando produtos' → 'A caminho do cliente', entered the correct code and clicked 'Confirmar entrega'. Page navigated back to /driver/home as expected and the delivered count incremented to 1 (Saldo do dia R$ 8,00).

issues: Non-blocking console warnings persist: 'shadow*' style props deprecated (use boxShadow); 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated, use style.pointerEvents' — react-native-web 0.20+ deprecations only, no functional impact

action_items: Both HIGH bugs from iter 1 are RESOLVED. No further action required for the reported items.
Optional polish (low priority): suppress react-native-web deprecation warnings (shadow*, pointerEvents) by migrating remaining components to boxShadow / style.pointerEvents.

Optional consistency: the same Alert.alert-callback anti-pattern still exists in acceptOrder/saveActualValue/sendInvoice/sendGoods (not failing because they don't depend on navigation), and in partner-signup success. Consider applying the same 'navigate/state-update first, alert after' pattern there for safety on web.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix in app/driver/order/[id].tsx confirmDelivery() correctly performs router.replace('/driver/home') BEFORE Alert.alert (lines 91-92) — this is the right approach for react-native-web.

Level strip JSX in app/driver/home.tsx (lines 72-79) correctly uses DRIVER_LEVELS[level] with testIDs driver-level-name and driver-level-limit; styles match the spec (Nível 2 • Parceiro confiável / Limite operacional: até R$ 150).
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data created. Full multi-role flow executed within a single Playwright session (shared localStorage). The order ord_1779116851146_100 with confirmation code 9244 was created and delivered during the test run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Working selectors verified end-to-end: login (login-email/login-password/login-submit), store-card-tosta-2, order-items-input, order-estimated-input, order-review-button, checkout-pay-button, confirmation-code (on /client/tracking), client-logout (on /client/home), driver-level-name, driver-level-limit, driver-available-{orderId}, driver-accept-button, driver-next-status (advances through 3 status changes to 'A caminho do cliente'), driver-code-input, driver-confirm-delivery, driver-logout. Use page.on('dialog', lambda d: d.accept()) — react-native-web maps Alert.alert to window.alert. For the 4-digit code extraction, after pay the page navigates to /client/tracking/{orderId}; testID is 'confirmation-code' (NOT 'client-confirmation-code').
rca_of_issue: Iter 1 HIGH bugs both fixed. BUG 1 RCA confirmed: prior code called router.replace inside Alert.alert button onPress, which is not invoked by react-native-web (it maps to window.alert and ignores per-button callbacks). Fix swaps the order: router.replace('/driver/home'); Alert.alert(...) — verified the redirect occurs (URL becomes /driver/home) and the order is moved to 'Entregues' (Saldo do dia incremented to R$ 8,00 for the R$ 8 delivery fee). BUG 2 RCA confirmed: missing JSX block in app/driver/home.tsx; styles (levelStrip/levelTitle/levelHint) and computed levelInfo were already present but never rendered. Fix added the View block at lines 72-79 with testIDs and shows 'Nível 2 • Parceiro confiável' / 'Limite operacional: até R$ 150' bordered with the level color.
